task_completion.description: Create attrs.xml defining the dotColor attribute for StatusDotView
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an editor tab named 'attrs.xml' containing XML code with <declare-styleable name="StatusDotView"> and an attribute <attr name="dotColor" format="color" />.

task_completion.description: Create the StatusDotView class in the correct package
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an editor tab named 'StatusDotView.kt' (or .java) with the package declaration 'package com.example.logisticsdashboard.views'.

task_completion.description: Implement logic to read the dotColor attribute and default to Red
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the StatusDotView code for attribute initialization, specifically retrieving 'dotColor' from a TypedArray and providing a fallback to a red color (e.g., Color.RED).

task_completion.description: Implement onDraw to render a filled circle centered within bounds
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the StatusDotView code for the 'onDraw' method, checking for 'canvas.drawCircle' with calculations for center X/Y (e.g., width/2, height/2) and radius based on view dimensions.

task_completion.description: Integrate StatusDotView into activity_main.xml with a specific color
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an editor tab named 'activity_main.xml' containing the custom view tag '<com.example.logisticsdashboard.views.StatusDotView' and an attribute setting the color, such as 'app:dotColor="..."'.

Look for the 'Build' tool window in Android Studio showing a successful build message (e.g., 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' or a green checkmark next to the build task), or the app running in the emulator displaying the custom view.

integrity.description: Agent used Android Studio to write code and build the project
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the screenshots show the Android Studio IDE being used to edit the files and run the build, rather than a basic text editor or terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not hardcode the color in the drawing logic
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the Paint object in StatusDotView uses the color retrieved from the XML attributes, rather than a hardcoded color directly in the onDraw method.
Check that the build success message is in the actual Android Studio Build/Run tool window, not a mocked text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/LogisticsDashboard with the package com.example.logisticsdashboard. The expected file paths relative to the project root are: attrs.xml at app/src/main/res/values/attrs.xml, StatusDotView.kt at app/src/main/java/com/example/logisticsdashboard/views/StatusDotView.kt, and activity_main.xml at app/src/main/res/layout/activity_main.xml.
